(Filing Fee For Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plaintiffs Psychological Records)Declaration Of Tania Rose In Support Of Plntf Richard Fiero'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plntf'S Psychological/Psychiatric Medical Records
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tania Rose, Rosecounsel, appearing for Plaintiff Richard Fiero, Telephone (415)724-1373;
Daniel R. Lentz, HansonBridgett, appearing for Defendant Golden Gate Bridge, et al; Telephone (415)777-3200;
No other appearance.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modifications as follows:

PLAINTIFF RICHARD FIERO'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Of Plaintiffs Psychological Records.

Plaintiff Richard Fiero's Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoenas to Cio Hernandez, Nancy Rhine, and Scott Barshak, MD, is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The Court, in its own discretion, will hear the late-filed Motion. Plaintiff has placed his mental state at issue in this case by alleging disability discrimination based upon preexisting depression. Furthermore, plaintiff has alleged that he "suffers and continues to suffer from "emotional distress" (Compl., par. 43). However, "while the filing of a lawsuit may implicitly bring about a partial waiver of one's constitutional right of associational privacy, the scope of such 'waiver' must be narrowly rather than expansively construed, so that plaintiffs will not be unduly deterred from instituting lawsuits by the fear of exposure of their private associational affiliations and activities." Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 842 quoting Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 859. Defendants must establish specific facts justifying inquiry into plaintiff's private life or show how such discovery would be relevant. Id. at 844. The subpoenas are limited to exclude billing records, pursuant to the agreement of the parties. The subpoenas are further limited to documents relating to depression (both pre and post termination) and symptoms of humiliation, emotional distress, and mental pain and anguish that post-date the termination. The documents provided should be marked "attorneys eyes only" and only verified by Defendant's attorneys for discovery purposes. Documents must be produced by September 12, 2018.

Defendants counsel submits a proposed order which tracks the tentative with modifications to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti.

After hearing an order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-19-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tania Rose, Esq. 415-777-3200 for Plaintiff Richard Fiero.
Diane Marie O'Malley, Esq. 415-777-3200 of HansonBridgett for Defendant DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, HIGHWAY, AND TRANSPORTATION.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares reasons for the tentative ruling. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE BRIDGE, HIGHWAY, AND TRANSPORTATION DISTRIC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication.

Defendant Golden Gate Bridge, Highway and Transportation District's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff Richard Fiero has submitted sufficient evidence to satisfy his minimal burden for a prima facie case for discrimination on the ground of mental disability. The District's evidence of the 2016 incident and the ensuing investigation shifted the burden to Mr. Fiero to produce substantial evidence creating a triable issue that the District's proffered legitimate non-discriminatory reasons of poor performance and dishonesty for the termination of Mr. Fiero's employment were pretextual. Construing the evidence in a light most favorable to Mr. Fiero and resolving all doubts in favor of Mr. Fiero, the evidence submitted on this motion shows that there is a triable issue of pretext based on 1) Mr. Swindler's question about Mr. Fiero's compliance with the condition imposed for the continuing validity of his license to submit to annual psychiatric examinations and 2) Mr. Swindler's awareness of the recent investigation into Mr. Fiero's stability in relation to workplace safety and guns. Based on reasonable inferences drawn from the foregoing evidence, a reasonable trier of fact could find that Mr. Fiero's depression was a substantial motivating reason for Mr. Swindler's decision to terminate Mr. Fiero's employment. (See Wallace v. County of Stanislaus (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 109, 129 (disability animus not required)).

Counsel for Mr. Fiero is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR #5525, vgross@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
